request.question: Treating IMR-90 fibroblasts with 100 nM rapamycin for 72 hours will reduce the number of p21-positive senescent cells by at least 40% compared to vehicle controls following hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence, due to mTORC1 inhibition reactivating autophagy-mediated clearance of damaged organelles.

hypothesis.intervention: Treating IMR-90 fibroblasts with 100 nM rapamycin for 72 hours
hypothesis.comparator_or_control: vehicle controls following hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence
hypothesis.threshold: at least 40% reduction compared to vehicle controls
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: mTORC1 inhibition reactivating autophagy-mediated clearance of damaged organelles
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: The intervention, biological system, comparator/control, numeric threshold, and mechanistic rationale are present. The measurable outcome is biologically defined but lacks a named assay method for quantifying p21-positive cells, and the vehicle/H2O2 senescence protocol is only partially specified, so scientist review is needed before CRO execution.
clarifying_questions: What assay or measurement method will be used to quantify p21-positive senescent cells (e.g., immunofluorescence microscopy, flow cytometry, or another defined assay)?
What vehicle control will be used and at what matched concentration/volume?
What hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence protocol will be used (e.g., H2O2 concentration, exposure duration, and recovery period)?
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: rapamycin IMR-90 p21 senescence autophagy mTORC1
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Treating IMR-90 fibroblasts with 100 nM rapamycin for 72 hours will reduce the number of p21-positive senescent cells by at least 40% compared to vehicle controls following hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence, due to mTORC1 inhibition reactivating autophagy-mediated clearance of damaged organelles.

references.title: mTOR regulates MAPKAPK2 translation to control the senescence-associated secretory phenotype
references.relevance_note: This paper is relevant because it directly links mTOR signaling—rapamycin’s target pathway—to senescence/SASP regulation, but the provided metadata does not show rapamycin treatment of IMR-90 cells or p21-positive cell counts after H2O2-induced senescence.

references.title: Apoptosis or senescence-like growth arrest: influence of cell-cycle position, p53, p21 and bax in H2O2 response of normal human fibroblasts.
references.relevance_note: This paper is relevant because it studies H2O2 responses in normal human fibroblasts and includes p21 in the context of senescence-like growth arrest, matching the biological stress context and outcome marker more closely than most papers, but not the rapamycin intervention.

references.title: Induction of replicative senescence biomarkers by sublethal oxidative stresses in normal human fibroblast.
references.relevance_note: This paper is relevant because it examines sublethal oxidative stress-induced senescence biomarkers in normal human fibroblasts, aligning with the H2O2-induced senescence model, but it does not indicate rapamycin/mTOR intervention or p21-positive cell quantification.

literature_qc.explanation: On the intervention dimension, none of the retrieved papers explicitly describe treating IMR-90 fibroblasts with rapamycin at 100 nM for 72 hours; paper [2] concerns mTOR regulation in senescence biology but the abstract/details provided do not establish the same intervention. On the biological system dimension, papers [3] and [4] involve normal human fibroblasts exposed to oxidative stress/H2O2-like senescence contexts, which are related to IMR-90 fibroblasts, while [5] is in colon cancer cells and less relevant. On the experiment type dimension, the retrieved set includes senescence modulation/biomarker studies and p21/H2O2 response work, but no paper clearly combines rapamycin treatment, IMR-90 fibroblasts, H2O2-induced senescence, and counting p21-positive senescent cells.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

evidence_claims.support_summary: Outcome is explicitly present in user hypothesis.

risks.description: Standard experimental risk — the approach has literature support but execution details should be reviewed before proceeding.
risks.mitigation: Confirm all materials, controls, and readiness criteria with a scientist before execution.

plan.objective: Determine whether 100 nM rapamycin for 72 h reduces the number of p21-positive senescent IMR-90 fibroblasts by at least 40% after hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence.
plan.experimental_design: IMR-90 fibroblasts will be seeded on coverslips in 24-well plates, exposed to sublethal H2O2 to induce senescence-like growth arrest, and treated for 72 h with 100 nM rapamycin or matched vehicle. The primary endpoint is the fraction of p21-positive nuclei measured by fixed-cell immunofluorescence across 6 wells per condition, with a ≥40% reduction versus H2O2 plus vehicle as the go/no-go threshold.
controls: IMR-90 fibroblasts exposed to 200 µM H2O2 for 60 min and treated with 0.001% v/v DMSO vehicle for 72 h.
IMR-90 fibroblasts cultured without H2O2 and treated with 0.001% v/v DMSO vehicle for 72 h.
vehicle controls following hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Equipment setup
Set a Thermo Scientific Heracell VIOS 160i incubator to 37°C, 5.0% CO2, and 95% relative humidity for IMR-90 culture.
Expected outcome: Incubator readout stabilizes at 37.0±0.3°C and 5.0±0.2% CO2 for 15 min.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 20 min | Reagent preparation
Dissolve 2.74 mg rapamycin in 300 µL sterile anhydrous DMSO to generate a 10 mM rapamycin stock at 25°C.
Expected outcome: Rapamycin solution is visually clear with no visible particulates in a 300 µL amber tube.
Safety: Rapamycin and DMSO can penetrate skin; handle with nitrile gloves and eye protection in a certified chemical hood.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Cell seeding
Seed 1.5×10^4 IMR-90 cells in 500 µL complete EMEM per well onto sterile collagen-coated 12 mm glass coverslips in a 24-well plate with 6 wells per condition.
Expected outcome: Each well contains 1.5×10^4 cells in 500 µL and the plate map assigns 6 wells each to no-H2O2 vehicle, H2O2 vehicle, H2O2 rapamycin, and H2O2 reference-control groups.
Safety: Handle human-derived IMR-90 cells under BSL-2 containment with disinfectant contact time of 10 min for liquid waste.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Senescence induction
Expose assigned senescence-induction wells to 500 µL serum-free EMEM containing 200 µM freshly diluted hydrogen peroxide for 60 min at 37°C and 5.0% CO2.
Expected outcome: No-H2O2 wells retain untreated medium and H2O2-designated wells complete a 60±2 min exposure without visible detachment exceeding 10% by phase-contrast inspection.
Safety: Hydrogen peroxide is an oxidizer; wear chemical splash goggles and discard diluted H2O2 into labeled oxidizer waste.

DAY 2 | 15 min | Post-stress wash
Wash each well once with 500 µL sterile DPBS at 25°C within 5 min of completing the H2O2 exposure.
Expected outcome: Residual H2O2-containing medium is removed and each coverslip remains fully covered by DPBS during the wash.
Safety: Treat aspirated liquid as BSL-2 chemical waste because it contains human cells and residual oxidizer.

DAY 2 | 25 min | Small-molecule treatment
Dose each well with 500 µL complete EMEM containing either 100 nM rapamycin with 0.001% v/v DMSO or 0.001% v/v DMSO vehicle at 37°C.
Expected outcome: Rapamycin wells contain 100 nM final rapamycin and every well contains 0.001% v/v final DMSO in 500 µL total medium.
Safety: Maintain aseptic technique in a BSL-2 cabinet to prevent contamination during the 72 h treatment window.

DAY 2 | 72 h | Drug exposure
Incubate the dosed 24-well plate for 72 h at 37°C, 5.0% CO2, and 95% relative humidity without medium exchange.
Expected outcome: Vehicle and rapamycin wells remain free of visible microbial contamination and retain ≥75% of the Day 2 adherent cell density by phase-contrast inspection.
Safety: Do not disturb the plate during the 72 h incubation except for a single 30 s visual contamination check through the incubator door window.

DAY 5 | 25 min | Fixation
Fix each coverslip with 500 µL 4% w/v paraformaldehyde in PBS for 15 min at 25°C.
Expected outcome: Cells are immobilized on coverslips with intact monolayer morphology and no visible coverslip drying.
Safety: Paraformaldehyde is toxic; handle in a certified chemical fume hood or ventilated enclosure and collect waste in a formaldehyde-labeled container.

DAY 5 | 1 h | p21 immunofluorescence staining
Incubate each fixed coverslip with 250 µL staining cocktail containing 0.1% v/v Triton X-100, 1% w/v BSA, 1 µg/mL Alexa Fluor 647-conjugated anti-p21 antibody, and 1 µg/mL DAPI for 60 min at 25°C protected from light.
Expected outcome: Each coverslip is fully covered by 250 µL staining cocktail and protected from ambient light for 60±5 min.
Safety: DAPI is a suspected mutagen; wear double nitrile gloves and collect DAPI-containing waste separately.

DAY 5 | 20 min | Post-stain wash
Wash each stained coverslip three times with 500 µL PBS for 5 min per wash at 25°C protected from light.
Expected outcome: Final wash liquid is visually clear and coverslips remain submerged with no drying artifacts.
Safety: Collect PBS washes as fluorescent dye waste because they contain residual DAPI and antibody conjugate.

DAY 5 | 1.5 h | High-content imaging
Acquire 9 non-overlapping fields per well using a Molecular Devices ImageXpress Micro Confocal system with a 20× objective, DAPI exposure of 50 ms, and Cy5 exposure of 200 ms.
Expected outcome: Each well yields 9 in-focus image fields with ≥150 DAPI-positive nuclei per well and no saturated Cy5 pixels in >1% of nuclei.

DAY 5 | 2 h | Quantitative image analysis and statistical interpretation
Compare the p21-positive nuclear fraction across 6 wells per condition using one-way ANOVA with Tukey’s post hoc test, Welch’s two-sided t-test for H2O2 vehicle versus H2O2 rapamycin, Shapiro-Wilk normality testing at α=0.05, and the predefined ≥40% reduction threshold.
Expected outcome: The pilot returns mean p21-positive percentages, 95% confidence intervals, p-values, and a proceed/repeat/stop call based on the ≥40% reduction and cytotoxicity criteria.
step_by_step_protocol.linked_to: Quantitative image analysis and statistical interpretation

assumptions: IMR-90 fibroblasts are mycoplasma-negative, below passage 25, and have a baseline p21-positive fraction ≤15% under vehicle-only culture.
A 200 µM hydrogen peroxide exposure for 60 min induces a reproducible senescence-like p21-positive phenotype in IMR-90 cells without reducing adherent cell count by >25%.
Rapamycin at 100 nM with a final DMSO concentration of 0.001% v/v is non-cytotoxic to IMR-90 cells during a 72 h post-H2O2 treatment window.
Nuclear p21 immunofluorescence intensity above a vehicle-only threshold identifies p21-positive senescent cells with sufficient dynamic range for automated image analysis.
decision_criteria: Proceed to a powered replicate study if 100 nM rapamycin reduces the p21-positive fraction by ≥40% versus H2O2 plus vehicle with Welch’s two-sided t-test p<0.05.
Repeat as a smaller optimization pilot if H2O2 plus vehicle produces <30% p21-positive cells or >25% loss of adherent nuclei versus no-H2O2 vehicle.
Stop this exact condition set if rapamycin-treated wells show >25% fewer DAPI-positive nuclei than H2O2 plus vehicle wells after 72 h.
reproducibility_notes: Use IMR-90 cells from a single authenticated lot, passage 18–24, with a mycoplasma PCR certificate dated within 30 days of seeding.
Use the same lot of EMEM, fetal bovine serum, H2O2, rapamycin, anti-p21 antibody, and collagen-coated coverslips across all wells in the pilot.
Record incubator CO2 calibration, ImageXpress illumination calibration, and pipette calibration identifiers before starting Day 1.
Keep final DMSO concentration at 0.001% v/v in every post-H2O2 condition, including negative and positive controls.
Acquire all fluorescence images with fixed exposure times and the same automated segmentation settings for every well.
feedback_incorporated: Reagent Preparation › materials (rating 5/5): something is off

materials.item_name: CO2 cell culture incubator (Thermo Scientific Heracell VIOS 160i or equivalent) daily use fee
materials.uncertainty_note: If already owned, this may be an internal facility recharge rather than a cash purchase.

materials.item_name: Class II biological safety cabinet use
materials.uncertainty_note: Many academic labs do not recharge BSC time; include as opportunity/equipment cost.

materials.item_name: Certified chemical fume hood / ventilated enclosure use
materials.uncertainty_note: Usually not billed directly, but required for rapamycin/DMSO and paraformaldehyde handling.

materials.item_name: Analytical microbalance for weighing rapamycin
materials.uncertainty_note: Internal charge varies by institution and calibration status.

materials.item_name: Rapamycin powder
materials.uncertainty_note: Pricing varies substantially by purity, supplier, and vial size; verify COA and cell-culture suitability.

materials.item_name: Sterile anhydrous DMSO, cell-culture grade
materials.uncertainty_note: Only a small fraction of the bottle is used, but sterile anhydrous DMSO is typically purchased as a bottle/ampoule.

materials.item_name: Amber low-binding microcentrifuge tubes for rapamycin stock
materials.uncertainty_note: Unit price depends on pack size and whether sterile/DNase-free tubes are selected.

materials.item_name: Sterile syringe filter or centrifugal filter for rapamycin stock if clarification/sterilisation is required
materials.uncertainty_note: Protocol only specifies a visually clear solution; filter may be omitted if using sterile components and aseptic technique.

materials.item_name: Nitrile gloves
materials.uncertainty_note: Double-gloving for DAPI and hazardous reagents increases consumption.

materials.item_name: Chemical splash goggles / eye protection allocation
materials.uncertainty_note: Usually reusable PPE already owned; shown as per-experiment allocation.

materials.item_name: Sterile serological pipettes, 5 mL and 10 mL assorted
materials.uncertainty_note: Actual use depends on whether media/reagents are prepared in bulk or per condition.

materials.item_name: Sterile filtered pipette tips, assorted P20/P200/P1000
materials.uncertainty_note: Tip use is operator-dependent; estimate includes extra tips for sterile technique and dilutions.

materials.item_name: Sterile 15 mL conical tubes
materials.uncertainty_note: Used for media, H2O2 dilution, washes, and staining cocktail preparation.

materials.item_name: Sterile 50 mL conical tubes
materials.uncertainty_note: Used for complete media and waste-compatible reagent preparation.

materials.item_name: Biohazard/chemical waste containers and absorbent bench pads allocation
materials.uncertainty_note: Waste disposal costs vary strongly by institution and hazardous-waste contractor.

materials.item_name: Vehicle control material: sterile anhydrous DMSO matched to 0.001% v/v final concentration
materials.quantity: vehicle for 18 wells plus dilution excess
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost is included under the DMSO reagent line; listed separately because it is essential for no-H2O2 and H2O2 vehicle controls.

materials.item_name: No-H2O2 negative control wells
materials.uncertainty_note: No additional purchased control reagent is required beyond medium and vehicle already costed.

materials.item_name: H2O2 vehicle senescence positive-control wells
materials.uncertainty_note: Control cost is represented by cells, medium, H2O2, and DMSO lines.

materials.item_name: Reference-control compound for H2O2 reference-control group, identity not specified in protocol
materials.quantity: 1 small vial, amount depends on selected reference
materials.uncertainty_note: The protocol names a reference-control group but does not identify the compound, concentration, or dosing vehicle; verify before ordering.

materials.item_name: IMR-90 human lung fibroblast cells
materials.quantity: 1 cryovial or existing working culture sufficient for 3.6×10^5 cells plus excess
materials.uncertainty_note: If an authenticated low-passage culture already exists, only maintenance cost applies; new vial pricing and shipping vary.

materials.item_name: Eagle's Minimum Essential Medium (EMEM), cell-culture grade
materials.uncertainty_note: Complete and serum-free EMEM are prepared from the same bottle; purchase volume is much larger than actual use.

materials.item_name: Fetal bovine serum for complete EMEM
materials.quantity: ~5 mL used; purchase/allocate from 500 mL bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost shown is pro-rated for ~50 mL complete medium at 10% FBS; FBS lot and origin cause large price differences.

materials.item_name: Penicillin-streptomycin solution, 100×
materials.quantity: ~0.5 mL used; pro-rated from 100 mL bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Some IMR-90 workflows omit antibiotics; included because complete EMEM commonly contains 1× pen-strep.

materials.item_name: L-glutamine or GlutaMAX supplement
materials.quantity: ~0.5 mL used; pro-rated from 100 mL bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Verify whether the selected EMEM already contains stable glutamine.

materials.item_name: Sterile collagen-coated 12 mm glass coverslips
materials.uncertainty_note: Pre-coated coverslips are relatively expensive; in-house collagen coating can reduce cost.

materials.item_name: Sterile tissue-culture treated 24-well plate
materials.uncertainty_note: Special optical-bottom plates are not required because imaging is through coverslips, unless the imager setup requires them.

materials.item_name: Trypsin-EDTA for IMR-90 detachment before seeding
materials.quantity: ~5 mL used; pro-rated from 100 mL bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Required if cells are passaged from a flask for seeding, though not explicitly listed in the steps.

materials.item_name: Trypan blue viability stain
materials.quantity: ~1 mL used; pro-rated from 100 mL bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Included for counting viable cells to seed 1.5×10^4 cells per well.

materials.item_name: Automated cell counter or hemocytometer with microscope use
materials.uncertainty_note: Manual hemocytometer cost is negligible if already owned; automated counters may have cartridge/recharge costs.

materials.item_name: Cell-counting slides or hemocytometer coverslips
materials.uncertainty_note: Only needed for automated cell counter or disposable counting chamber workflow.

materials.item_name: Inverted phase-contrast microscope use for cell density/detachment checks
materials.uncertainty_note: Often shared with no recharge; included because visual inspection is explicitly required.

materials.item_name: Hydrogen peroxide solution, 30% w/w analytical grade
materials.quantity: ~10 mL diluted working solution; purchase 100 mL bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Only microlitres are used, but stock is commonly purchased as a 100 mL bottle.

materials.item_name: Serum-free EMEM for H2O2 exposure
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost included in the EMEM bottle line under Cell seeding.

materials.item_name: Sterile DPBS without calcium/magnesium
materials.quantity: ~15 mL used for post-stress wash; purchase/allocate from 500 mL bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost is pro-rated; many labs hold DPBS as shared stock.

materials.item_name: Complete EMEM containing 100 nM rapamycin or 0.001% DMSO
materials.supplier: In-house preparation from Gibco/Sigma/LC Laboratories reagents
materials.uncertainty_note: Component costs are included under EMEM, FBS, supplements, rapamycin, and DMSO lines.

materials.item_name: Pipettes P20, P200, P1000 use/calibration allocation
materials.uncertainty_note: Usually owned by the lab; allocation covers wear/calibration rather than purchase.

materials.item_name: 4% paraformaldehyde in PBS, methanol-free fixative
materials.quantity: 12 mL required; purchase 20 mL ampoules or 100 mL bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Ready-made ampoules are more expensive but safer and more reproducible than preparing PFA powder.

materials.item_name: Triton X-100
materials.quantity: 6 µL required in staining cocktail; allocate from 100 mL bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Actual experiment consumption is negligible; bottle purchase may already be shared stock.

materials.item_name: Bovine serum albumin (BSA), immunostaining grade
materials.quantity: 60 mg required for 6 mL staining cocktail; allocate from 10 g bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost is pro-rated; verify grade is suitable for immunofluorescence blocking.

materials.item_name: Alexa Fluor 647-conjugated anti-p21 antibody
materials.uncertainty_note: Antibody price and required concentration vary by clone, validation status, and conjugation; test dilution may change cost.

materials.item_name: DAPI nuclear stain
materials.quantity: 6 µg required in staining cocktail; purchase 1 mg vial or 1 mL stock
materials.uncertainty_note: Only a tiny amount is used, but DAPI is generally purchased as a vial/stock solution.

materials.item_name: Light-protective foil or black plate cover
materials.uncertainty_note: Needed to protect Alexa Fluor 647 and DAPI from ambient light.

materials.item_name: PBS for post-stain washes
materials.quantity: 36 mL required for 3 × 500 µL × 24 wells; pro-rated from 500 mL bottle
materials.uncertainty_note: Cost is pro-rated; DPBS/PBS bottle may be the same shared stock as used in the post-stress wash if compatible.

materials.item_name: Molecular Devices ImageXpress Micro Confocal high-content imaging system with 20× objective
materials.uncertainty_note: Core facility charges commonly range from ~50–150 EUR/hour depending on support and confocal mode.

materials.item_name: Immersion-free imaging plate holder/adaptor for 24-well coverslip plate
materials.uncertainty_note: Often included with instrument use; may be unnecessary if the existing plate carrier supports 24-well plates.

materials.item_name: Image analysis/statistics workstation with licensed software or R/Python environment
materials.uncertainty_note: Open-source analysis can reduce direct software cost; commercial analysis modules or GraphPad licenses may be charged by the facility.

line_items.uncertainty_note: Antibody price and required concentration vary by clone, validation status, and conjugation; test dilution may change cost.

line_items.item_name: DAPI nuclear stain
line_items.quantity: 6 µg required in staining cocktail; purchase 1 mg vial or 1 mL stock
line_items.uncertainty_note: Only a tiny amount is used, but DAPI is generally purchased as a vial/stock solution.

line_items.item_name: Light-protective foil or black plate cover
line_items.uncertainty_note: Needed to protect Alexa Fluor 647 and DAPI from ambient light.

line_items.item_name: PBS for post-stain washes
line_items.quantity: 36 mL required for 3 × 500 µL × 24 wells; pro-rated from 500 mL bottle
line_items.uncertainty_note: Cost is pro-rated; DPBS/PBS bottle may be the same shared stock as used in the post-stress wash if compatible.

line_items.item_name: Molecular Devices ImageXpress Micro Confocal high-content imaging system with 20× objective
line_items.uncertainty_note: Core facility charges commonly range from ~50–150 EUR/hour depending on support and confocal mode.

line_items.item_name: Immersion-free imaging plate holder/adaptor for 24-well coverslip plate
line_items.uncertainty_note: Often included with instrument use; may be unnecessary if the existing plate carrier supports 24-well plates.

line_items.item_name: Image analysis/statistics workstation with licensed software or R/Python environment
line_items.uncertainty_note: Open-source analysis can reduce direct software cost; commercial analysis modules or GraphPad licenses may be charged by the facility.

line_items.item_name: Laboratory disinfectant suitable for BSL-2 liquid waste contact time, e.g. Virkon or 10% bleach
line_items.uncertainty_note: Cost depends on institutional disinfection policy and whether bleach is prepared from shared stock.

uncertainty_notes: Major cost drivers are the IMR-90 source, directly conjugated anti-p21 antibody, rapamycin vial size, and high-content imager/core-facility recharge; these should be verified with institutional purchasing quotes.
Several reagents are costed as pro-rated usage from standard lab bottles, while others are costed at minimum purchasable package size; actual cash outlay will differ depending on existing stock.

The protocol lists an H2O2 reference-control group but does not specify the reference compound, dose, or vehicle, so that control line is a placeholder requiring scientific and purchasing clarification before ordering.

phases.phase_name: Day 0: Incubator setup and rapamycin stock preparation
bottlenecks: Incubator CO2/temperature/RH stabilization or recent decontamination cycle delaying use
Rapamycin stock solubility/particulates requiring repeat preparation
Chemical hood availability for DMSO/rapamycin handling

phases.phase_name: Day 1: IMR-90 cell seeding on collagen-coated coverslips
dependencies: Day 0: Incubator setup and rapamycin stock preparation
bottlenecks: IMR-90 cells not at suitable confluence or passage number
Uneven cell suspension/seeding causing well-to-well variability
Biosafety cabinet availability and pre-coated coverslip/plate readiness

phases.phase_name: Day 1-2: Cell attachment and recovery after seeding
dependencies: Day 1: IMR-90 cell seeding on collagen-coated coverslips
bottlenecks: Slow or uneven attachment to coverslips
Unexpected detachment or low viability after seeding
Contamination detected during pre-treatment visual check

phases.phase_name: Day 2: H2O2 senescence induction, wash, and rapamycin/vehicle dosing
dependencies: Day 1-2: Cell attachment and recovery after seeding
bottlenecks: Fresh H2O2 dilution accuracy and peroxide potency
Timing coordination to keep 60±2 min exposure and rapid wash consistent across wells
Excessive cell detachment after oxidative stress preventing continuation

dependencies: Day 2: H2O2 senescence induction, wash, and rapamycin/vehicle dosing
bottlenecks: Incubator excursions in CO2, humidity, or temperature over the 72 h exposure
Microbial contamination during no-medium-change incubation
Rapamycin/DMSO treatment-related cytotoxicity below acceptance threshold

phases.phase_name: Day 5: Fixation, p21/DAPI immunofluorescence staining, and washes
bottlenecks: PFA handling area or waste container availability
Coverslip drying or loss during liquid exchanges
Light-sensitive DAPI/Alexa 647 staining variability or antibody reagent shortage

dependencies: Day 5: Fixation, p21/DAPI immunofluorescence staining, and washes
bottlenecks: ImageXpress booking/availability and objective/filter configuration
Autofocus failures on coverslips or uneven staining requiring reacquisition
Signal saturation or low nuclei count per well requiring exposure adjustment

phases.phase_name: Day 5-6: Quantitative image analysis, statistics, and results write-up
bottlenecks: Image segmentation QC for p21-positive nuclear fraction
Export/import issues from high-content imaging software
Biological variability or failed normality/cytotoxicity criteria requiring repeat pilot

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 40% reduction compared to vehicle controls improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: Determine whether 100 nM rapamycin for 72 h reduces the number of p21-positive senescent IMR-90 fibroblasts by at least 40% after hydrogen peroxide-induced senescence.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
